And Allah is better as regards reward in comparison to your [Fir'aun's (Pharaoh)] reward, and more lasting (as regards punishment in comparison to your punishment)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We have believed in our Lord, so that He may forgive us our sins, and the magic you have compelled us to practice. God is Better, and more Lasting.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We believe in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and also forgive us the practice of magic to which you had compelled us. Allah is the Best and He alone will abide."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Verily, we have believed in our Lord, that He may forgive us our faults, and the magic to which you did compel us. And Allah is better as regards reward in comparison to your reward, and more lasting."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! we believe in our Lord, that He may forgive us our sins and the magic unto which thou didst force us. Allah is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We have indeed believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our offences and the magic you compelled us to perform. Allah is better and more lasting.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We believe in our Lord so that He forgives us our sins and the sorcery you have forced us to practice. Allah is Better, and Everlasting'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and what you compelled us [to do] of magic. And Allah is better and more enduring."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have faith in our Lord so that He will forgive our sins and our magical performances that you forced us to show. God is better than all things and His rewards last longer."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely we believe in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and the magic to which you compelled us; and Allah is better and more abiding.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: we have believed in our Lord so that He may forgive us our sins and forgive us the sorcery that you have forced us to practice. God is the best and the most abiding."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "For us, we have believed in our Lord: may He forgive us our faults, and the magic to which thou didst compel us: for Allah is Best and Most Abiding."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna amanna birabbina liyaghfira lana khatayana wama akrahtana AAalayhi mina alssihri waAllahu khayrun waabqa

74.arabic: إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَأْتِ رَبَّهُۥ مُجْرِمًا فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely for him who comes before his Lord a sinner shall be Hell, where he will neither die nor live.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the one who comes guilty to his Lord – so undoubtedly for him is hell; neither dying nor living in it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Whosoever comes unto his Lord a sinner, for him awaits Gehenna wherein he shall neither die nor live.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily whosoever cometh unto his lord as a Culprit, for him is Hell wherein he will neither die nor live.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! Whoever comes to his Lord as a Mujrim (criminal, polytheist, disbeliever in the Oneness of Allah and His Messengers, sinner, etc.), then surely, for him is Hell, therein he will neither die nor live.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Whoever comes to his Lord guilty, for him is Hell, where he neither dies nor lives.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The truth is that Hell awaits him who comes to his Lord laden with sin; he shall neither die in it nor live.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal, then surely, for him is Hell, wherein he will neither die nor live.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! whoso cometh guilty unto his Lord, verily for him is hell. There he will neither die nor live.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whoever comes to his Lord laden with guilt, for him shall be hell, where he will neither live nor die.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Gehenna (Hell) awaits whosoever comes before his Lord as a sinner, there he shall neither die nor live.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal - indeed, for him is Hell; he will neither die therein nor live.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The dwelling place of one who comes into the presence of his Lord as a criminal will be hell wherein he will never die nor enjoy his life.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Whoever comes to his Lord (being) guilty, for him is surely hell; he shall not die therein, nor shall he live.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Indeed, he who comes to his Lord a sinner shall be consigned to Hell; he shall neither die therein nor live.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily he who comes to his Lord as a sinner (at Judgment),- for him is Hell: therein shall he neither die nor live.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innahu man yati rabbahu mujriman fainna lahu jahannama la yamootu feeha wala yahya

75.arabic: وَمَن يَأْتِهِۦ مُؤْمِنًا قَدْ عَمِلَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلدَّرَجَـٰتُ ٱلْعُلَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But whoever comes before Him a believer having done good deeds, will be raised to higher stations --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the one who presents himself as a believer before Him, having done good deeds – so for them are the high ranks.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And whoso comes unto Him a believer having done deeds of righteousness, those -- for them await the most sublime degrees;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And whosoever cometh unto Him as a believer, and he hath done righteous works---then these! for them are ranks high.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But whoever comes to Him (Allah) as a believer (in the Oneness of Allah, etc.), and has done righteous good deeds, for such are the high ranks (in the Hereafter),

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But whoever comes to Him a believer, having worked righteousness—these will have the highest ranks.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But he who comes to Him with faith and righteous works shall be exalted to high ranks,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But whoever comes to Him as a believer, and has done righteous good deeds, for such are the high ranks,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But whoso cometh unto Him a believer, having done good works, for such are the high stations;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But whoever comes to Him with faith and he has done righteous deeds, for such shall be the highest ranks

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But for whosoever comes before Him as a believer and having done good works there awaits the most highest degree;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But whoever comes to Him as a believer having done righteous deeds - for those will be the highest degrees [in position]:

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: One who comes into the presence of his Lord with faith and righteous deeds

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And whoever comes to Him a believer (and) he has done good deeds indeed, these it is who shall have the high ranks,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But he who comes to Him as a believer, having done good deeds, shall be exalted to the highest ranks,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But such as come to Him as Believers who have worked righteous deeds,- for them are ranks exalted,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman yatihi muminan qad AAamila alssalihati faolaika lahumu alddarajatu alAAula

76.arabic: جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Gardens of Eden with rippling streams, where he will live for ever. This is the recompense of those who achieve integrity.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Everlasting Gardens of Eden beneath which rivers flow, abiding in them for ever; and this is the reward of one who became pure.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Gardens of Eden, underneath which rivers flow, therein dwelling forever; that is the recompense of the self-purified.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Gardens Everlasting whereunder rivers flow: abiders therein; that is the meed of him who hath purified himself.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: 'Adn (Edn) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), under which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever: such is the reward of those who purify themselves [(by abstaining from all kinds of sins and evil deeds) which Allah has forbidden and by doing all that which Allah has ordained)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The Gardens of Perpetuity, beneath which rivers flow, dwelling therein forever. That is the reward for him who purifies himself.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and shall live for ever in everlasting Gardens beneath which rivers flow. Such will be the reward of those who purify themselves.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: `Adn Gardens, under which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever, and such is the reward of those who purify themselves.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Gardens of Eden underneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide for ever. That is the reward of him who groweth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —the Gardens of Eden, with streams running in them, to abide in them [forever], and that is the reward of him who keeps pure.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: living for ever in the Gardens of Eden, underneath which rivers flow. Such shall be the recompense of he who purifies himself.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. And that is the reward of one who purifies himself.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: will be rewarded by high status in the gardens of Eden wherein streams flow. Such will be the reward of those who purify themselves.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The gardens of perpetuity, beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein; and this is the reward of him who has purified himself.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: he will abide forever in the Gardens of eternity, through which rivers flow. That is the recompense for those who purify themselves.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Gardens of Eternity, beneath which flow rivers: they will dwell therein for aye: such is the reward of those who purify themselves (from evil).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Jannatu AAadnin tajree min tahtiha alanharu khalideena feeha wathalika jazao man tazakka

77.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِى فَٱضْرِبْ لَهُمْ طَرِيقًا فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ يَبَسًا لَّا تَخَـٰفُ دَرَكًا وَلَا تَخْشَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We commanded Moses: "Journey by night with Our creatures, and strike a dry path for them through the sea. Do not fear being overtaken, nor have dread of any thing."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We divinely inspired Moosa that, “Journey with My bondmen in a part of the night and strike a dry path in the sea for them, you shall have no fear of Firaun getting to you, nor any danger.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Also We revealed unto Moses, 'Go with My servants by night; strike for them a dry path in the sea, fearing not overtaking, neither afraid.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly We revealed unto Musa, saying: depart in night with my bondmen, and make for them in the sea a parth dry: thou Shalt fear neither overtaking, nor shalt thou be afraid.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed We inspired Musa (Moses) (saying): "Travel by night with Ibadi (My slaves) and strike a dry path for them in the sea, fearing neither to be overtaken [by Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] nor being afraid (of drowning in the sea)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We inspired Moses: “Travel by night with My servants, and strike for them a dry path across the sea, not fearing being overtaken, nor worrying.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Most certainly We revealed to Moses: "Proceed with My servants in the night and strike for them a dry path in the sea. Have no fear of being overtaken, nor be afraid of treading through the sea."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed We revealed to Musa: "Travel by night with My servants and strike a dry path for them in the sea, fearing neither to be overtaken nor being afraid."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And verily We inspired Moses, saying: Take away My slaves by night and strike for them a dry path in the sea, fearing not to be overtaken, neither being afraid (of the sea).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We revealed to Moses, [saying], ‘Set out with My servants at night, and strike out for them a dry path through the sea. Do not be afraid of being overtaken, and have no fear [of getting drowned].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: To Moses we also revealed: 'Set forth with My worshipers by night and strike for them a dry path in the sea. Do not fear that you will be overtaken, neither be afraid'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We had inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken [by Pharaoh] nor be afraid [of drowning]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent revelations to Moses telling him, "Travel with My servants during the night and strike a dry road across the sea (for them). Have no fear of being overtaken (by the Pharaoh) nor of anything else".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly We revealed to Musa, saying: Travel by night with My servants, then make for them a dry path in the sea, not fearing to be overtaken, nor being afraid.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent a revelation to Moses saying, "Take away My servants by night and strike for them a dry path through the sea. Have no fear of being overtaken and do not be afraid."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We sent an inspiration to Moses: "Travel by night with My servants, and strike a dry path for them through the sea, without fear of being overtaken (by Pharaoh) and without (any other) fear."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad awhayna ila moosa an asri biAAibadee faidrib lahum tareeqan fee albahri yabasan la takhafu darakan wala takhsha

78.arabic: فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ بِجُنُودِهِۦ فَغَشِيَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْيَمِّ مَا غَشِيَهُمْ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then the Pharaoh followed them with his army, but the sea overpowered and engulfed them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So Firaun went after them with his army – therefore the sea enveloped them, the way it did.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Pharaoh followed them with his hosts, but they were overwhelmed by the sea;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then Fir'awn followed them with his hosts; and there came upon them of the sea that which came upon them.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then Fir'aun (Pharaoh) pursued them with his hosts, but the sea-water completely overwhelmed them and covered them up.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Pharaoh pursued them with his troops, but the sea overwhelmed them, and completely engulfed them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Pharaoh pursued them with his hosts, but they were fully overwhelmed by the sea.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then Fir`awn pursued them with his hosts, but the sea [Al-Yamm] completely overwhelmed them and covered them up.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then Pharaoh followed them with his hosts and there covered them that which did cover them of the sea.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then Pharaoh pursued them with his troops, whereat they were engulfed by what engulfed them of the sea.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Pharaoh pursued them with his legions so they were overwhelmed from the sea with that which drowned them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers, and there covered them from the sea that which covered them,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Pharaoh and his army chased Moses and his people but were drowned by the sea.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Firon followed them with his armies, so there came upon them of the sea that which came upon them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Pharaoh pursued them with his hosts, but they were submerged by the sea, which was destined to overwhelm them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then Pharaoh pursued them with his forces, but the waters completely overwhelmed them and covered them up.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FaatbaAAahum firAAawnu bijunoodihi faghashiyahum mina alyammi ma ghashiyahum

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The Pharaoh had led his people astray, and did not rightly guide them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And Firaun led his people astray, and did not guide them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: so Pharaoh had led his people astray, and was no guide to them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Fir'awn led his nation astray, and guided them not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And Fir'aun (Pharaoh) led his people astray, and he did not guide them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Pharaoh misled his people, and did not guide them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Pharaoh led his people astray; he did not guide them aright.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And Fir`awn led his people astray, and he did not guide them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Pharaoh led his folk astray, he did not guide them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For Pharaoh had misled his nation, and did not guide them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide [them].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Pharaoh and his people had gone away from guidance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Firon led astray his people and he did not guide (them) aright.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: For Pharaoh had led his people astray and did not guide them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Pharaoh led his people astray instead of leading them aright.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waadalla firAAawnu qawmahu wama hada

80.arabic: يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ قَدْ أَنجَيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ عَدُوِّكُمْ وَوَٰعَدْنَـٰكُمْ جَانِبَ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلْأَيْمَنَ وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy, and made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount, and sent down for you manna and quails,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O Descendants of Israel, indeed We rescued you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of Mount Tur, and sent down Manna and Salwa upon you.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy; and We made covenant with you upon the right side of the Mount, and sent down on you manna and quails:

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Children of Israel; We delivered you from your enemy, and treated with you on the right side of the mount and sent down upon you the manna and the quails.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount, and We sent down to you Al-Manna and quails,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O Children of Israel! We have delivered you from your enemy, and promised you by the right side of the Mount, and sent down to you manna and quails.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Children of Israel! We saved you from your enemy and made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount and sent down on you manna and quails,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount, and We sent down to you manna and quail,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and we made a covenant with you on the holy mountain's side, and sent down on you the manna and the quails,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We appointed with you a tryst on the right side of the Mount and We sent down to you manna and quails:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Children of Israel! We saved you from your enemies and made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mountain. We sent down manna and quails.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O Children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy, and We made an appointment with you at the right side of the mount, and We sent down to you manna and quails,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Children of Israel, We saved you from your enemy and promised to settle you on the right side of the peaceful Mount Tur (Sinai)

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O children of Israel! indeed We delivered you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the blessed side of the mountain, and We sent to you the manna and the quails.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemies and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount. We sent down manna and quails for you,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We made a Covenant with you on the right side of Mount (Sinai), and We sent down to you Manna and quails:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya banee israeela qad anjaynakum min AAaduwwikum wawaAAadnakum janiba alttoori alaymana wanazzalna AAalaykumu almanna waalssalwa

81.arabic: كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبِى ۖ وَمَن يَحْلِلْ عَلَيْهِ غَضَبِى فَقَدْ هَوَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (And said): "Eat of the good things We have given you for food, and do not exceed the bounds (of law) in this, or My wrath will surely fall upon you; and he who incurs My wrath will fall into the abyss.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Eat the good clean things We have provided you, and do not exceed the limits in respect of it causing My wrath to descend upon you; and indeed the one on whom My wrath descended, has fallen.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Eat of the good things wherewith We have provided you; but exceed not therein, or My anger shall alight on you; and on whomsoever My anger alights, that man is hurled to ruin.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Saying: eat of the clean things wherewith We have provided you, and wax not exorbitant in respect thereof, lest My wrath alight on you; and upon whomsoever My wrath alighteth, he surely perisheth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Saying) eat of the Taiyibat (good lawful things) wherewith We have provided you, and commit no oppression therein, lest My Anger should justly descend on you. And he on whom My Anger descends, he is indeed perished.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Eat of the good things We have provided for you, but do not be excessive therein, lest My wrath descends upon you. He upon whom My wrath descends has fallen.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: saying: "Partake of the good things that We have provided for you, but do not transgress lest My wrath fall upon you; for he upon whom My wrath falls is ruined.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Saying) eat of the Tayyibat wherewith We have provided you, and commit no transgression or oppression therein, lest My anger should justly descend on you. And he on whom My anger descends, he is indeed perished.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Saying): Eat of the good things wherewith We have provided you, and transgress not in respect thereof lest My wrath come upon you: and he on whom My wrath cometh, he is lost indeed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘Eat of the good things We have provided you, but do not overstep the bounds therein, lest My wrath should descend on you, and he on whom My wrath descends certainly perishes.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Eat of the good things with which We have provided you and do not transgress therein lest My Anger should fall upon you, and upon whosoever My Anger falls has assuredly fallen,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you and do not transgress [or oppress others] therein, lest My anger should descend upon you. And he upon whom My anger descends has certainly fallen."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and We sent you manna and quails.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Eat of the good things We have given you for sustenance, and be not inordinate with respect to them, lest My wrath should be due to you, and to whomsoever My wrath is due be shall perish indeed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Eat from the wholesome things with which We have provided you but do not transgress, lest you should incur My wrath." [We said], "He that incurs My wrath shall surely be ruined.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Saying): "Eat of the good things We have provided for your sustenance, but commit no excess therein, lest My Wrath should justly descend on you: and those on whom descends My Wrath do perish indeed!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kuloo min tayyibati ma razaqnakum wala tatghaw feehi fayahilla AAalaykum ghadabee waman yahlil AAalayhi ghadabee faqad hawa

82.arabic: وَإِنِّى لَغَفَّارٌ لِّمَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا ثُمَّ ٱهْتَدَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet I am gracious to him who repents and believes, and does the right, and follows the straight path.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed I am Most Oft Forgiving for him who repented and accepted faith and did good deeds, and then remained upon guidance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet I am All-forgiving to him who repents and believes, and does righteousness, and at last is guided.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And verily I am the Most Forgiving unto whomsoever repenteth and believeth and worketh righteously, and thereafter letteth himself remain guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And verily, I am indeed Forgiving to him who repents, believes (in My Oneness, and associates none in worship with Me) and does righteous good deeds, and then remains constant in doing them, (till his death).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And I am Forgiving towards him who repents, believes, acts righteously, and then remains guided.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But I am indeed Most Forgiving to him who repents and believes and does righteous works and keeps to the Right Way."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And verily, I am indeed forgiving to him who repents, believes and does righteous deeds, and then Ihtada.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And lo! verily I am Forgiving toward him who repenteth and believeth and doeth good, and afterward walketh aright.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed I forgive those who repent, become faithful, act righteously, and follow guidance.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but to whosoever repents, believes and does good deeds, and is at last guided, I am Forgiving'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But indeed, I am the Perpetual Forgiver of whoever repents and believes and does righteousness and then continues in guidance.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: I allowed you to consume the pure sustenance which We had given you but not to become rebels, lest you become subject to My wrath. Whoever becomes subject to My wrath will certainly be destroyed. I am All-forgiving to the righteously striving believers who repent and follow the right guidance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And most surely I am most Forgiving to him who repents and believes and does good, then continues to follow the right direction.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But I am most forgiving towards him who turns in repentance and believes and acts righteously and follows the right path."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But, without doubt, I am (also) He that forgives again and again, to those who repent, believe, and do right, who,- in fine, are ready to receive true guidance."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wainnee laghaffarun liman taba waamana waAAamila salihan thumma ihtada

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: What made you hurry away, O Moses, from your people?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And why did you come in haste ahead of your people, O Moosa?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'What has sped thee far from thy people, Moses?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And what hath made thee hasten from thy people, O Musa?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And what made you hasten from your people, O Musa (Moses)?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And what made you rush ahead of your people, O Moses?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: "But, O Moses, what has made you come in haste from your people?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And what made you hasten from your people, O Musa

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (it was said): What hath made thee hasten from thy folk, O Moses?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [God said,] ‘O Moses, what has prompted you to hasten ahead of your people?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Moses, why have you come with such haste from your nation'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Allah] said, "And what made you hasten from your people, O Moses?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Lord asked, "Moses, what made you attend your appointment with Me before your people?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And what caused you to hasten from your people, O Musa?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [When Moses was upon the Mount, God said,] "O Moses, why have you come with such haste from your people?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (When Moses was up on the Mount, Allah said:) "What made thee hasten in advance of thy people, O Moses?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama aAAjalaka AAan qawmika ya moosa

84.arabic: قَالَ هُمْ أُو۟لَآءِ عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِى وَعَجِلْتُ إِلَيْكَ رَبِّ لِتَرْضَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "They are right behind me. I have hastened to You, O Lord, so that You may be pleased."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He submitted, “They are here, just behind me – and O my Lord, I hastened towards You, in order to please You.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'They are upon my tracks,' Moses said. 'I have hastened, Lord, only that I may please Thee.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: those! they are close on my footstep, and hastened to Thee, O my Lord; that Thou mightest be wellpleased.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He said: "They are close on my footsteps, and I hastened to You, O my Lord, that You might be pleased."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “They are following in my footsteps; and I hurried on to You, my Lord, that you may be pleased.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He said: "They are close behind me, and I hastened to You, Lord, that You may be pleased with me."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He said: "They are close on my footsteps, and I hastened to You, O my Lord, that You might be pleased."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: They are close upon my track. I hastened unto Thee, my Lord, that Thou mightest be well pleased.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘They are close upon my heels, and I hurried on to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Moses replied: 'They are following me. My Lord, I made haste only so that I might please You'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You be pleased."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Moses replied, "They are just behind me. I came earlier to seek Your pleasure".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: They are here on my track and I hastened on to Thee, my Lord, that Thou mightest be pleased.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "They are following in my footsteps, while I have hastened to You, my Lord, to please You."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He replied: "Behold, they are close on my footsteps: I hastened to thee, O my Lord, to please thee."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala hum olai AAala atharee waAAajiltu ilayka rabbi litarda

85.arabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّا قَدْ فَتَنَّا قَوْمَكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "We have put your people on trial in your absence; and Sameri has led them astray."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “We have therefore tried your people after you came, and Samri has led them astray.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said He, 'We have tempted thy people since thou didst leave them. The Samaritan has misled them into error.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: verily We have tempted thy people after thee, and the Samiri hath led them astray.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Allah) said: "Verily! We have tried your people in your absence, and As-Samiri has led them astray."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “We have tested your people in your absence, and the Samarian misled them.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Said He: "Verily We tested your people in your absence and the Samiri led them astray."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Allah) said: "Verily, We have tried your people in your absence, and As-Samiri has led them astray."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: Lo! We have tried thy folk in thine absence, and As-Samiri hath misled them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘Indeed We tried your people in your absence, and the Samiri has led them astray.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He (Allah) said: 'We tempted your nation in your absence, and the Samaritan has misled them into error'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Allah] said, "But indeed, We have tried your people after you [departed], and the Samiri has led them astray."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Lord said, "We tested your people after you left them and the Samiri made them go astray."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: So surely We have tried your people after you, and the Samiri has led them astray.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But God said, "We have tested your people in your absence. The Samiri has led them astray."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Allah) said: "We have tested thy people in thy absence: the Samiri has led them astray."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala fainna qad fatanna qawmaka min baAAdika waadallahumu alssamiriyyu

86.arabic: فَرَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ غَضْبَـٰنَ أَسِفًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَلَمْ يَعِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا ۚ أَفَطَالَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْعَهْدُ أَمْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن يَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُم مَّوْعِدِى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So Moses returned to his people full of anger and regret. "O my people," he said, "did not your Lord make you a better promise? Did the time of covenant seem too long to you? Or did you wish the wrath of your Lord to fall upon you that you broke the promise you had made to me?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So Moosa turned back to his people, angry and grieving; he said, “O my people, had not your Lord given you a good promise? Did a long time pass away for you, or did you wish that your Lord’s wrath come upon you, therefore you broke your promise with me?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then Moses returned very angry and sorrowful to his people, saying, 'My people, did your Lord not promise a fair promise to you? Did the time of the covenant seem so long to you, or did you desire that anger should alight on you from your Lord, so that you failed in your tryst with me?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So Musa returned unto his people, indignant and sorrowful. He said: O my people! promised there not your Lord unto you an excellent promise! Lasted then the covenant too long for you, or desired ye that the wrath from your Lord should alight on you, so that ye failed to keep my appointment?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then Musa (Moses) returned to his people in a state of anger and sorrow. He said: "O my people! Did not your Lord promise you a fair promise? Did then the promise seem to you long in coming? Or did you desire that wrath should descend from your Lord on you, so you broke your promise to me (i.e. disbelieving in Allah and worshipping the calf)?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So Moses returned to his people, angry and disappointed. He said, “O my people, did your Lord not promise you a good promise? Was the time too long for you? Or did you want wrath from your Lord to descend upon you, so you broke your promise to me?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Moses returned to his people full of wrath and grief, and said: "My people! Has your Lord not made good an excellent promise He made to you? And has a long time passed since those promises were fulfilled? Or was it to incur the wrath of your Lord that you broke your promise with me?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then Musa returned to his people in a state of anger and sorrow [Asif]. He said: "O my people! Did not your Lord promise you a fair promise Did then the promise seem to you long in coming Or did you desire that wrath should descend from your Lord on you, that you broke your promise to me"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then Moses went back unto his folk, angry and sad. He said: O my people! Hath not your Lord promised you a fair promise? Did the time appointed then appear too long for you, or did ye wish that wrath from your Lord should come upon you, that ye broke tryst with me?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Thereupon Moses returned to his people, indignant and grieved. He said, ‘O my people! Did your Lord not give you a true promise? Did the period [of my absence] seem too long to you? Or did you desire that your Lord’s wrath should descend on you and so you failed your tryst with me?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: With great anger and sorrow, Moses returned to his nation. 'My nation' he said, 'did your Lord not make you a fine promise? Did the time of the covenant seem long to you? Or did you desire that the anger of your Lord should fall upon you so that you failed in your coming to my appointment'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said, "O my people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Then, was the time [of its fulfillment] too long for you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord descend upon you, so you broke your promise [of obedience] to me?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Moses, sad and angry, returned to his people saying, "My people, did not the Lord make you a gracious promise? Why did you disregard your appointment with me? Was it because of the long time or did you want to become subject to the wrath of your Lord?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So Musa returned to his people wrathful, sorrowing. Said he: O my people! did not your Lord promise you a goodly promise: did then the time seem long to you, or did you wish that displeasure from your Lord should be due to you, so that you broke (your) promise to me?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Moses returned to his people in anger and great sorrow. He said, "My people, did your Lord not make you a handsome promise? Was my absence too long for you? Did you desire that your Lord's wrath should descend upon you, when you broke your promise to me?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So Moses returned to his people in a state of indignation and sorrow. He said: "O my people! did not your Lord make a handsome promise to you? Did then the promise seem to you long (in coming)? Or did ye desire that Wrath should descend from your Lord on you, and so ye broke your promise to me?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FarajaAAa moosa ila qawmihi ghadbana asifan qala ya qawmi alam yaAAidkum rabbukum waAAdan hasanan afatala AAalaykumu alAAahdu am aradtum an yahilla AAalaykum ghadabun min rabbikum faakhlaftum mawAAidee

87.arabic: قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَخْلَفْنَا مَوْعِدَكَ بِمَلْكِنَا وَلَـٰكِنَّا حُمِّلْنَآ أَوْزَارًا مِّن زِينَةِ ٱلْقَوْمِ فَقَذَفْنَـٰهَا فَكَذَٰلِكَ أَلْقَى ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said: "We did not break our promise to you of our own will, but we were made to carry the loads of ornaments belonging to the people, which we threw (into the fire), and so did Sameri.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “We did not renege on our promise to you on our own will, but we were made to carry the burdens of ornaments of the people, so we cast them – and similarly did Samri cast.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'We have not failed in our tryst with thee,' they said, 'of our volition; but we were loaded with fardels, even the ornaments of the people, and we cast them, as the Samaritan also threw them, into the fire.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: we failed not to keep thy appointment of our own authority, but we were laden with burthens of the people's ornaments; then we threw them, and Thus Samiri cast down.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "We broke not the promise to you, of our own will, but we were made to carry the weight of the ornaments of the [Fir'aun's (Pharaoh)] people, then we cast them (into the fire), and that was what As-Samiri suggested."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “We did not break our promise to you by our choice, but we were made to carry loads of the people’s ornaments, and we cast them in. That was what the Samarian suggested.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They answered: "We did not break our promise with you out of our own volition; but we were laden with the load of people's ornaments, and we simply threw them down(into the fire), and the Samiri also threw down something,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "We broke not the promise to you, of our own will, but we were made to carry the weight of the ornaments of the people, then we cast them, and that was what As-Samiri suggested."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: We broke not tryst with thee of our own will, but we were laden with burdens of ornaments of the folk, then cast them (in the fire), for thus As-Samiri proposed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘We did not fail our tryst with you of our own accord, but we were laden with the weight of those people’s ornaments, and we cast them [into the fire] and so did the Samiri.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They replied: 'We have not failed in our promise to you through our choosing. We were laden with fardels, even the ornaments of the nation, and threw them just as the Samaritan had thrown them (into the fire),

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "We did not break our promise to you by our will, but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people [of Pharaoh], so we threw them [into the fire], and thus did the Samiri throw."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They replied, "We did not go against our promise with you out of our own accord. We were forced to carry people's ornaments. We threw them away and so did the Samiri.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: We did not break (our) promise to you of our own accord, but we were made to bear the burdens of the ornaments of the people, then we made a casting of them, and thus did the Samiri suggest.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They answered, "We did not break our promise to you of our own accord, but we had to carry loads of the people's ornaments and so we threw them [into the fire] for that was what the Samiri suggested,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "We broke not the promise to thee, as far as lay in our power: but we were made to carry the weight of the ornaments of the (whole) people, and we threw them (into the fire), and that was what the Samiri suggested.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ma akhlafna mawAAidaka bimalkina walakinna hummilna awzaran min zeenati alqawmi faqathafnaha fakathalika alqa alssamiriyyu

88.arabic: فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلًا جَسَدًا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌ فَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ وَإِلَـٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِىَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then he produced the image of a calf which mooed like a cow. And they said: 'This is your god and the god of Moses (whom) he has neglected."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He therefore made a calf for them – a lifeless body, making sounds like a cow – so they said, “This is your God and the God of Moosa; whereas Moosa has forgotten.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: (Then he brought out for them a Calf, a mere body that lowed; and they said, 'This is your god, and the god of Moses, whom he has forgotten.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And he produced for them a calf: a body with a low. Then they Said: this is your god and the god of Musa, and that he forgat.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then he took out (of the fire) for them a statue of a calf which seemed to low. They said: "This is your ilah (god), and the ilah (god) of Musa (Moses), but [Musa (Moses)] has forgotten (his god).'"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So he produced for them a calf—a mere body which lowed. And they said, “This is your god, and the god of Moses, but he has forgotten.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and brought out of there (from the molten gold) the effigy of a calf that lowed." The people cried out: "This is your deity and the deity of Moses, whom Moses has forgotten."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then he took out for them a calf which was mooing. They said: "This is your god, and the god of Musa," but he had forgotten.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then he produced for them a calf, of saffron hue, which gave forth a lowing sound. And they cried: This is your god and the god of Moses, but he hath forgotten.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then he produced for them a calf—a [lifeless] body with a low—and they said, This is your god and the god of Moses, so he forgot!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and made a calf for them, a figure that lowed. "This," they said, "is your god and the god of Moses but whom he has forgotten."

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Then the Samiri forged the body of a motionless calf which gave out a hollow sound." The people said, "This is your Lord and the Lord of Moses whom he (Moses) forgot to mention".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So he brought forth for them a calf, a (mere) body, which had a mooing sound, so they said: This is your god and the god of Musa, but he forgot.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: then he forged a calf for them -- an image producing a lowing sound. They said, "This is your deity, the deity of Moses; he has forgotten it."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Then he brought out (of the fire) before the (people) the image of a calf: It seemed to low: so they said: This is your god, and the god of Moses, but (Moses) has forgotten!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faakhraja lahum AAijlan jasadan lahu khuwarun faqaloo hatha ilahukum wailahu moosa fanasiya

89.arabic: أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَلَّا يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِمْ قَوْلًا وَلَا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Did they not see that it did not give them any answer, nor had it power to do them harm or bring them gain?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So do they not see that it does not answer to any of their speech? And has no power to cause them any harm or benefit?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What? Did they not see that thing returned no speech unto them, neither had any power to hurt or profit them?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Observed they not that it returned not unto them a word, and owned not for them hurt or profit?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Did they not see that it could not return them a word (for answer), and that it had no power either to harm them or to do them good?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Did they not see that it cannot return a word to them, and has no power to harm them or benefit them?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did they not see that it did not return a word to them, and had no power either to hurt them or to cause them any benefit?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Did they not see that it could not return them a word (for answer), and that it had no power either to harm them or to do them good

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: See they not, then, that it returneth no saying unto them and possesseth for them neither hurt nor use?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Did they not see that it did not answer them, nor could it bring them any benefit or harm?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: What! Did they not see that it did not speak a word to them in return, and for them it could own neither harm nor benefit?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Did they not see that it could not return to them any speech and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Did they not consider that the calf could not give them any answer, nor it could harm or benefit them?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! could they not see that it did not return to them a reply, and (that) it did not control any harm or benefit for them?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Why did they not see that it could not give them any response and had no power to harm or benefit them?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Could they not see that it could not return them a word (for answer), and that it had no power either to harm them or to do them good?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afala yarawna alla yarjiAAu ilayhim qawlan wala yamliku lahum darran wala nafAAan

90.arabic: وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَـٰرُونُ مِن قَبْلُ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى وَأَطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرِى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Aaron had indeed told them earlier: "O my people, you are being only misled with this. Surely your Lord is Ar-Rahman. So follow me and obey my command."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And undoubtedly Haroon had told them before it that, “O my people – you have needlessly fallen into trial because of this; and indeed your Lord is the Most Gracious, therefore follow me and obey my command.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet Aaron had aforetime said to them, 'My people, you have been tempted by this thing, no more; surety your Lord is the All-merciful; therefore follow me, and obey my commandment!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly Harun had said unto them afore: O my people! ye are only being tempted thereby; and verily your Lord is the Compassionate; so follow me and obey my command.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And Harun (Aaron) indeed had said to them beforehand: "O my people! You are being tried in this, and verily, your Lord is (Allah) the Most Beneficent, so follow me and obey my order."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Aaron had said to them before, “O my people, you are being tested by this. And your Lord is the Merciful, so follow me, and obey my command.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Certainly Aaron had said to them even before (the return of Moses): "My people, you were fallen into error because of the calf. Surely your Lord is Most Compassionate; so follow me and obey my command."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And Harun indeed had said to them beforehand: "O my people! You are being tried in this, and verily, your Lord is (Allah) the Most Gracious, so follow me and obey my order."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Aaron indeed had told them beforehand: O my people! Ye are but being seduced therewith, for lo! your Lord is the Beneficent, so follow me and obey my order.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Aaron had certainly told them earlier, ‘O my people! You are only being tested by it. Indeed your Lord is the All-beneficent. So follow me and obey my command!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Aaron had said to them before: 'My nation, you have been tempted by it. Your Lord is the Merciful. Follow me and obey my order'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Aaron had told them before, "My people, you are deceived by the calf. Your Lord is the Beneficent God. Follow me and obey my orders".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly Haroun had said to them before: O my people! you are only tried by it, and surely your Lord is the Beneficent Allah, therefore follow me and obey my order.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Aaron had already told them, "O my people! You are only being tested by this. Your Lord is the All Merciful, so follow me and obey my command."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Aaron had already, before this said to them: "O my people! ye are being tested in this: for verily your Lord is (Allah) Most Gracious; so follow me and obey my command."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad qala lahum haroonu min qablu ya qawmi innama futintum bihi wainna rabbakumu alrrahmanu faittabiAAoonee waateeAAoo amree

91.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَن نَّبْرَحَ عَلَيْهِ عَـٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They said "So long as Moses does not come back we are not going to give it up, and we will remain devoted to it."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “We will continue to squat* before it, till Moosa returns to us.” (* Continue worshipping it.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'We will not cease,' they said, 'to cleave to it, until Moses returns to us.')

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: we shall by no means cease to be assiduous to it until there returneth to us Musa.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "We will not stop worshipping it (i.e. the calf), until Musa (Moses) returns to us."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “We will not give up our devotion to it, until Moses returns to us.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But they answered: "By no means shall we cease to worship it until Moses returns to us."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "We will not stop worshipping it, until Musa returns to us."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: We shall by no means cease to be its votaries till Moses return unto us.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They had said, ‘We will keep on clinging to it until Moses returns to us.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They replied: 'We will not stop; we will cling to it until Moses returns to us.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "We shall continue worshipping the calf until Moses comes back."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: We will by no means cease to keep to its worship until Musa returns to us.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They replied, "We shall not cease to worship it until Moses returns to us."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They had said: "We will not abandon this cult, but we will devote ourselves to it until Moses returns to us."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo lan nabraha AAalayhi AAakifeena hatta yarjiAAa ilayna moosa

92.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰهَـٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذْ رَأَيْتَهُمْ ضَلُّوٓا۟

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But (Moses) said: "O Aaron, when you saw that they had gone astray, what hindered you

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Said Moosa, “O Haroon – what prevented you when you saw them going astray?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Moses said, 'What prevented thee, Aaron, when thou sawest them in error,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Musa said: O Harun! what prevented thee, when thou sawest them going astray.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: [Musa (Moses)] said: "O Harun (Aaron)! What stopped you when you saw them going astray;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (After rebuking his people) Moses turned to Aaron and said: "Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Musa) said: "O Harun! What prevented you when you saw them going astray;"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He (Moses) said: O Aaron! What held thee back when thou didst see them gone astray,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O Aaron! What kept you, when you saw them going astray,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He (Moses) said to Aaron: 'When you saw them in error, what prevented you,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Moses] said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Then Moses asked Aaron, "What made you not follow me when you saw them in error?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (Musa) said: O Haroun! what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Moses said to Aaron, "What prevented you, when you saw that they had gone astray,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Moses) said: "O Aaron! what kept thee back, when thou sawest them going wrong,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya haroonu ma manaAAaka ith raaytahum dalloo

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: From coming after me? Did you not disobey my command?" (And Moses pulled him by the hair).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “That you did not come after me? So did you disobey my order?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: so that thou didst not follow after me? Didst thou then disobey my commandment?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That thou followedst me not! Hast thou disobeyed my command?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "That you followed me not (according to my advice to you)? Have you then disobeyed my order?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: From following me? Did you disobey my command?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: from following my way? Have you disobeyed my command?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "That you followed me not (according to my advice to you) Have you then disobeyed my order"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That thou followedst me not? Hast thou then disobeyed my order?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: from following me? Did you disobey my command?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: from following after me, did you disobey my order'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Did you disobey my orders?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So that you did not follow me? Did you then disobey my order?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: from following me? Why did you disobey my command?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "From following me? Didst thou then disobey my order?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alla tattabiAAani afaAAasayta amree

94.arabic: قَالَ يَبْنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأْخُذْ بِلِحْيَتِى وَلَا بِرَأْسِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقْتَ بَيْنَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمْ تَرْقُبْ قَوْلِى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "O son of my mother," (Aaron cried), do not pull me by my beard or my hair! I was really afraid you may say that I had created a rift among the children of Israel, and did not pay heed to your command."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O son of my mother, do not clutch my beard nor the hair on my head; I feared that you may say, ‘You have caused a division among the Descendants of Israel and did not wait for my advice.’”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Son of my mother,' Aaron said, 'take me not by the beard, or the head! I was fearful that thou wouldst say, "Thou hast divided the Children of Israel, and thou hast not observed my word."

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Harun said: O my mother's son! hold me not by my beard nor by my head verily feared that thou wouldst say, thou hast caused a division among the Children of Israel and hast not guarded my Word.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He [Harun (Aaron)] said: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard, nor by my head! Verily, I feared lest you should say: 'You have caused a division among the Children of Israel, and you have not respected my word!' "

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “Son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or my head. I feared you would say, `You have caused division among the Children of Israel, and did not regard my word.'“

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Aaron answered: "Son of my mother! Do not seize me with my beard, nor by (the hair of) my head. I feared that on returning you might say: 'You sowed discord among the Children of Israel, and did not pay heed to my words.'"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He (Harun) said: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard, nor by my head! Verily, I feared lest you should say: `You have caused a division among the Children of Israel, and you have not respected my word!"'

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O son of my mother! Clutch not my beard nor my head! I feared lest thou shouldst say: Thou hast caused division among the Children of Israel, and hast not waited for my word.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O son of my mother! Do not grab my beard or my head! I feared lest you should say, ‘‘You have caused a rift among the Children of Israel and did not heed my word [of advice].’’’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Son of my mother' he replied, 'Do not seize my beard nor my head. I was afraid that you might say: "You have divided the Children of Israel and did not uphold my word."

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word.' "

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Aaron replied, "Son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or head. I was afraid that you might consider me responsible for causing discord among the children of Israel and would not pay attention to my words."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O son of my mother! seize me not by my beard nor by my head; surely I was afraid lest you should say: You have caused a division among the children of Israel and not waited for my word.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Aaron said, "Son of my mother! Do not seize me by my beard nor by my head. I was afraid that you would say, 'You have caused dissension among the Children of Israel and did not pay heed to my words.'"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Aaron) replied: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard nor by (the hair of) my head! Truly I feared lest thou shouldst say, 'Thou has caused a division among the children of Israel, and thou didst not respect my word!'"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya bna omma la takhuth bilihyatee wala birasee innee khasheetu an taqoola farraqta bayna banee israeela walam tarqub qawlee

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Moses asked: "O Sameri, what was the matter?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Said Moosa, “And what is your explanation, O Samri?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Moses said, 'And thou, Samaritan, what was thy business?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Musa said: what was thy object, O Samiri?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: [Musa (Moses)] said: "And what is the matter with you. O Samiri? (i.e. why did you do so?)"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “What do you have to say, O Samarian?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Moses said: "What, then, is your case, O Samiri?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Musa) said: "And what is the matter with you. O Samiri"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Moses) said: And what hast thou to say, O Samiri?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘What is your business, O Samiri?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'You, Samaritan' said he (Moses), 'what was your business'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Moses] said, "And what is your case, O Samiri?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Moses asked, "Samiri, what were your motives?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: What was then your object, O Samiri?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Moses said, "What was the matter with you, Samiri?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Moses) said: "What then is thy case, O Samiri?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala fama khatbuka ya samiriyyu

96.arabic: قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةً مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذْتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتْ لِى نَفْسِى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He said: "I saw what they did not see. I picked up a handful of dust from the messenger's tracks and threw it in, for the idea seemed attractive to me."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “I witnessed what the people did not witness – I therefore took a handful from the tracks* of the angel, then threw it** – and this is what seemed pleasing to my soul.” (* The marks left behind by the mount of Angel Jibreel. ** Into the mouth of the calf.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'I beheld what they beheld not,' he said, 'and I seized a handful of dust from the messenger's track, and cast it into the thing. So my soul prompted me.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: l saw that which they saw not; so I seized a handful from the footstep of the messenger, and then cast it; Thus my soul embellished the affair to me.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Samiri) said: "I saw what they saw not, so I took a handful (of dust) from the hoof print of the messenger [Jibrael's (Gabriel) horse] and threw it [into the fire in which were put the ornaments of the Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people, or into the calf]. Thus my inner-self suggested to me."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “I saw what they did not see, so I grasped a handful from the Messenger’s traces, and I flung it away. Thus my soul prompted me.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He answered: "I saw what the people did not see. So I took a handful of dust from the trail of the Messenger, and I flung it (into the fire). Thus did my mind prompt me."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Samiri) said: "I saw what they saw not, so I took a (Qabdah) handful (of dust) from the (hoof) print of the messenger and threw it. Thus my inner self suggested to me."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: I perceived what they perceive not, so I seized a handful from the footsteps of the messenger, and then threw it in. Thus my soul commended to me.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘I saw what they did not see. I took a handful [of dust] from the messenger’s trail and threw it. That is how my soul prompted me.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He replied: 'I saw what they did not see and seized a handful of dust from the foot print of the messenger and so I cast it this my soul prompted me to do'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He replied, "I had the skill (of carving) which they did not have. I followed some of the Messengers's (Moses) tradition, but I then ignored it. Thus, my soul prompted me (to carve a golden calf with an artificial hollow sound).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: I saw (Jibreel) what they did not see, so I took a handful (of the dust) from the footsteps of the messenger, then I threw it in the casting; thus did my soul commend to me

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "I perceived what they did not see. So I took a handful [of dust] from the footprint of the Messenger and threw it in [the calf]. That is what my inner self prompted me to do."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He replied: "I saw what they saw not: so I took a handful (of dust) from the footprint of the Messenger, and threw it (into the calf): thus did my soul suggest to me."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala basurtu bima lam yabsuroo bihi faqabadtu qabdatan min athari alrrasooli fanabathtuha wakathalika sawwalat lee nafsee

97.arabic: قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَّن تُخْلَفَهُۥ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِى ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عَاكِفًا ۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ نَسْفًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (Moses) said: "Go hence! All your life you are (cursed) to say: 'Do not touch me; and a threat hangs over you which you will not be able to escape. Look at your god to whom you are so attached: We shall verily burn it, and disperse its ashes into the sea.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Said Moosa, “Therefore go away, for in this life your punishment is that you exclaim ‘Do not touch!’* And indeed for you is a time appointed, which you cannot break; and look at your deity, in front of which you remained squatting the whole day; we swear we will surely burn it and, smashing it into bits, discharge it into the river.” (* He was cursed with a disease.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Depart!' said Moses. 'It shall be thine all this life to cry "Untouchable!" And thereafter a tryst awaits thee thou canst not fail to keep Behold thy god, to whom all the day thou wast cleaving! We will surely burn it and scatter its ashes into the sea.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Musa said: begone thou! verily it shall be thine in life to say no contact; and verily thine is an appointment which thou shalt by no means fail. And look at thy god to which thou hast been assidauous; we shall surely burn it, and we shall scatter it into the sea a wide scattering.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Then go away! And verily, your (punishment) in this life will be that you will say: "Touch me not (i.e. you will live alone exiled away from mankind); and verily (for a future torment), you have a promise that will not fail. And look at your ilah (god), to which you have been devoted. We will certainly burn it, and scatter its particles in the sea."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “Begone! Your lot in this life is to say, ‘No contact.’ And you have an appointment that you will not miss. Now look at your god that you remained devoted to—we will burn it up, and then blow it away into the sea, as powder.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Moses said: "Be gone, then. All your life you shall cry: 'Untouchable.' There awaits a term for your reckoning that you cannot fail to keep. Now look at your god that you devotedly adored: We shall burn it and scatter its remains in the sea.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Musa said: "Then go away! And verily, your (punishment) in this life will be that you will say: `Touch me not;' and verily, you have a promise that will not fail. And look at your god to which you have been devoted. We will certainly burn it, and scatter its particles in the sea."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Moses) said: Then go! and lo! in this life it is for thee to say: Touch me not! and lo! there is for thee a tryst thou canst not break. Now look upon thy god of which thou hast remained a votary. Verily we will burn it and will scatter its dust over the sea.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘Begone! It shall be your [lot] throughout life to say, ‘‘Do not touch me!’’ Indeed, there is a tryst for you which you will not fail to keep! Now look at your god to whom you went on clinging. We will burn it down and then scatter it[s ashes] into the sea.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Begone! Your lot in this life is to cry: "untouchable!' said he (Moses). 'An appointment awaits you that you cannot fail to keep. Look at your god which you clung to indeed we will burn it and scatter its ashes upon the sea'

translations.author: Saheeh International
