translations.text: But whosoever turns away from My Reminder, verily, for him is a life of hardship, and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of Resurrection."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But he who turneth away from remembrance of Me, his will be a narrow life, and I shall bring him blind to the assembly on the Day of Resurrection.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But whoever disregards My remembrance, his shall be a wretched life, and We shall raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but whosoever turns away from My remembrance, his life shall be narrow and on the Day of Resurrection We shall raise him blind'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed life, and We will gather him on the Day of Resurrection blind."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Whoever ignores My guidance will live a woeful life and will be brought in Our presence blind on the Day of Judgment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And whoever turns away from My reminder, his shall be a straitened life, and We will raise him on the day of resurrection, blind.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but whoever turns away from My reminder, will lead a straitened existence and on the Day of Judgement We shall raise him up blind

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But whosoever turns away from My Message, verily for him is a life narrowed down, and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of Judgment."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman aAArada AAan thikree fainna lahu maAAeeshatan dankan wanahshuruhu yawma alqiyamati aAAman

125.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِىٓ أَعْمَىٰ وَقَدْ كُنتُ بَصِيرًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He will ask: "O Lord, why have you raised me blind when I was able to see?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He will say, “O my Lord, why have You raised me blind, whereas I was sighted?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He shall say, 'O my Lord, why hast thou raised me blind, and I was wont to see?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He will say: O my Lord! why hast Thou gathered me sightless whereas surely I have been a seer.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He will say:"O my Lord! Why have you raised me up blind, while I had sight (before)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will say, “My Lord, why did You raise me blind, though I was seeing?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: where-upon he will say: "Lord! Why have You raised me blind when I had sight in the world?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He will say: "O my Lord! Why have you raised me up blind, while I had sight (before)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He will say: My Lord! Wherefor hast Thou gathered me (hither) blind, when I was wont to see?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He will say, ‘My Lord! Why have You raised me blind, though I used to see?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'My Lord' he will say: 'why have You raised me blind when I was able to see'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He will say, "My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He will say, "My Lord, why have you brought me back to life blind; before I could see?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He shall say: My Lord! why hast Thou raised me blind and I was a seeing one indeed?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and he will ask, "Lord, why have You raised me up blind, while I possessed sight before?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He will say: "O my Lord! why hast Thou raised me up blind, while I had sight (before)?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala rabbi lima hashartanee aAAma waqad kuntu baseeran

126.arabic: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ أَتَتْكَ ءَايَـٰتُنَا فَنَسِيتَهَا ۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ تُنسَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (God) will say: "Because Our signs came to you, but you disregarded them. So shall We disregard you this day."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He will say, “Similarly did Our signs come to you but you forgot them; and in the same way, nobody will heed you today.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God shall say, 'Even so it is. Our signs came unto thee, and thou didst forget them; and so today thou art forgotten.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah will say: Thus came Our signs Unto thee and thou ignoredst them; so that wise today thou shalt be ignored.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Allah) will say: "Like this, Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) came unto you, but you disregarded them (i.e. you left them, did not think deeply in them, and you turned away from them), and so this Day, you will be neglected (in the Hell-fire, away from Allah's Mercy)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will say, “Just as Our revelations came to you, and you forgot them, today you will be forgotten.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He will say: "Even so it is. Our Signs came to you and you ignored them. So shall you be ignored this Day."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Allah) will say: "Like this Our Ayat came unto you, but you disregarded them, and so this Day, you will be neglected."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He will say: So (it must be). Our revelations came unto thee but thou didst forget them. In like manner thou art forgotten this Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He will say: ‘So it is. Our signs came to you, but you forgot them, and so you will be forgotten today.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He (Allah) will say: 'It is so, Our verses came to you and you forgot them. So this Day you are forgotten'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Allah] will say, "Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Lord will say, "This is true. But just as you forgot Our revelations that had come to you, so, too, are you forgotten on this day."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He will say: Even so, Our communications came to you but you neglected them; even thus shall you be forsaken this day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God will say, "Just as Our signs came to you and you ignored them, so will you on this Day be ignored."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Allah) will say: "Thus didst Thou, when Our Signs came unto thee, disregard them: so wilt thou, this day, be disregarded."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala kathalika atatka ayatuna fanaseetaha wakathalika alyawma tunsa

127.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى مَنْ أَسْرَفَ وَلَمْ يُؤْمِنۢ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ۚ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And that is how We requite him who is extravagant and does not believe the signs of his Lord; and surely the punishment of the Hereafter is far more severe and persistent.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And this is how We reward him who transgresses and does not accept faith in the signs of his Lord; and indeed the punishment of the Hereafter is the most severe and more lasting.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So We recompense him who is prodigal and believes not in the signs of his Lord; and the chastisement of the world to come is more terrible and more enduring.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thus We requite him who trespasseth and believeth not in the signs of his Lord, and surely the torment of the Hereafter is most severe and most lasting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And thus do We requite him who transgresses beyond bounds [i.e. commits the great sins and disobeys his Lord (Allah) and believes not in His Messengers, and His revealed Books, like this Quran, etc.], and believes not in the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of his Lord, and the torment of the Hereafter is far more severe and more lasting.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Thus We recompense him who transgresses and does not believe in the revelations of his Lord. The punishment of the Hereafter is more severe, and more lasting.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus do We requite him who transgresses and does not believe in the signs of your Lord (during the life of the world); and surely the punishment of the Hereafter is even more terrible and more enduring.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And thus do We requite him who transgresses beyond bounds and believes not in the Ayat of his Lord; and the torment of the Hereafter is far more severe and more lasting.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Thus do We reward him who is prodigal and believeth not the revelations of his Lord; and verily the doom of the Hereafter will be sterner and more lasting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Thus do We requite those who transgress and do not believe in the signs of their Lord, and the punishment of the Hereafter is severer and more lasting.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: In this way We recompense the prodigal who disbelieves the verses of his Lord. But the punishment of the Everlasting Life is more terrible and everlasting.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And thus do We recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more enduring.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Thus We recompense those who are unjust and have no faith in Our revelations. The torment in the life to come will be more severe and last longer.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And thus do We recompense him who is extravagant and does not believe in the communications of his Lord, and certainly the chastisement of the hereafter is severer and more

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Thus We shall reward the transgressor who denies the signs of his Lord. But the suffering of the life to come is more terrible and more lasting.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And thus do We recompense him who transgresses beyond bounds and believes not in the Signs of his Lord: and the Penalty of the Hereafter is far more grievous and more enduring.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakathalika najzee man asrafa walam yumin biayati rabbihi walaAAathabu alakhirati ashaddu waabqa

128.arabic: أَفَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ يَمْشُونَ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلنُّهَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Did they not learn from the many generations that We destroyed before them, whose habitations they now frequent? Verily there are signs in this for men of understanding.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So did they not gain guidance from (knowing) how many generations We have destroyed before them, among whose dwellings they walk? Indeed in it are signs for men of intellect.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Is it not a guidance to them, how many generations We destroyed before them in whose dwelling-places they walk? Surely in that are signs for men possessing reason.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Hath it not served as guidance to them how many a generation We have destroyed before them, amidst whose dwellings they Walk! Verily therein are signs for men of sagacity.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is it not a guidance for them (to know) how many generations We have destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they walk? Verily, in this are signs indeed for men of understanding.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is it not instructive to them, how many generations before them We destroyed, in whose settlements they walk? Surely in that are signs for people of understanding.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did they not find any guidance in the fact that We destroyed many nations in whose ruined dwelling-places they now walk about? Surely there are many Signs in them for people endowed with wisdom.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is it not a guidance for them (to know) how many generations We have destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they walk Verily, in this are signs indeed for men of understanding.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is it not a guidance for them (to know) how many a generation We destroyed before them, amid whose dwellings they walk? Lo! therein verily are signs for men of thought.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Does it not dawn upon them how many generations We have destroyed before them, amid [the ruins of] whose dwellings they walk? There are indeed signs in this for those who have reason.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is it not a guidance to them, how many generations We destroyed before them in whose dwelling places they walk? Surely, in this there are signs for thoseof reason.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then, has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Is it not a warning for them to see how many generations living before them We destroyed and how they are now walking in their ruins? In this there is the evidence (of the Truth) for the people of reason.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Does it not then direct them aright how many of the generations In whose dwelling-places they go about We destroyed before them? Most surely there are signs in this for those endowed with understanding.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they not learn a lesson from Our destruction of many generations before them in whose dwelling-places they walk about? Surely in this are signs for men of understanding.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it not a warning to such men (to call to mind) how many generations before them We destroyed, in whose haunts they (now) move? Verily, in this are Signs for men endued with understanding.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afalam yahdi lahum kam ahlakna qablahum mina alqurooni yamshoona fee masakinihim inna fee thalika laayatin liolee alnnuha

129.arabic: وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَكَانَ لِزَامًا وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If the decree (of respite) had not been pronounced by your Lord, (the inevitable judgement would have ensued); but a term is fixed (for everything).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had not a command of your Lord been passed, then the punishment would have gripped them – and had a term not been appointed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And but for a word that preceded from thy Lord, and a stated term, it had been fastened.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had not a word from thy Lord gone forth, and a term determined, it must necessarily have come.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And had it not been for a Word that went forth before from your Lord, and a term determined, (their punishment) must necessarily have come (in this world).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Were it not for a word that issued from your Lord, the inevitable would have happened, but there is an appointed term.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Were it not for a word already gone from your Lord, the decree (of their destruction) would have come to pass.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And had it not been for a Word that went forth before from your Lord, and a term determined, (their punishment) must necessarily have come (in this world).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And but for a decree that had already gone forth from thy Lord, and a term already fixed, the judgment would have been inevitable (in this world).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were it not for a prior decree of your Lord and a specified time, [their doom] would have attended [their guilt].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Except for a Word that preceded from your Lord, and a stated term, it had been fastened.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, punishment would have been an obligation [due immediately], and [if not for] a specified term [decreed].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had not the word of your Lord been decreed (otherwise), the unbelievers deserved immediate punishment. The appointed time for their punishment will inevitably come.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And had there not been a word (that had) already gone forth from your Lord and an appointed term, it would surely have been made to cleave (to them).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But for a pre-ordained Word from your Lord, and a term [of respite] already fixed, immediate punishment would inevitably have taken place.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Had it not been for a Word that went forth before from thy Lord, (their punishment) must necessarily have come; but there is a Term appointed (for respite).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walawla kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika lakana lizaman waajalun musamman

130.arabic: فَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ غُرُوبِهَا ۖ وَمِنْ ءَانَآئِ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So you bear with patience what they say, and sing the praises of your Lord before the rising and setting of the sun, and honour Him in the watches of the night, and then at the two ends of day, that you may find acceptance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), patiently forbear upon their speech, and praising your Lord proclaim His Purity, before the sun rises and before it sets; and proclaim His Purity at some times of the night and at the two ends of the day, in the hope that you be pleased. (*With the great reward from your Lord)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So be thou patient under what they say, and proclaim thy Lord's praise before the rising of the sun, and before its setting, and proclaim thy Lord's praise in the watches of the night, and at the ends of the day; haply thou wilt be well-pleasing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So bear thou patiently that which they say, and hallow the praise of thy Lord before the rising of the sun and before the setting thereof. And hallow Him in part of the night and the ends of the day, haply thou wilt be pleased.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So bear patiently (O Muhammad SAW) what they say, and glorify the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun, and before its setting, and during some of the hours of the night, and at the sides of the day (an indication for the five compulsory congregational prayers), that you may become pleased with the reward which Allah shall give you.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So bear patiently what they say, and celebrate the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun, and before its setting. And during the hours of the night glorify Him, and at the borders of the day, that you may be satisfied.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So bear patiently with what they say. Glorify your Lord, praising Him before sunrise and before sunset, and in the watches of the night, and glorify Him and at the ends of the day that you may attain to happiness.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So bear patiently what they say, and glorify the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun, and before its setting, and during some hours of the night, and at the ends of the day, that you may become pleased with the reward which Allah shall give you.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefor (O Muhammad), bear with what they say, and celebrate the praise of thy Lord ere the rising of the sun and ere the going down thereof. And glorify Him some hours of the night and at the two ends of the day, that thou mayst find acceptance.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So be patient with what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the sunset, and glorify Him in watches of the night and at the day’s ends, that you may be pleased.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Therefore, be patient with what they say, and exalt with the praise of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset. And in the watches of the night and at the edges of the day, exalt Him, so that you will be pleasing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So be patient over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during periods of the night [exalt Him] and at the ends of the day, that you may be satisfied.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), have patience with what they say, glorify your Lord, and always praise Him before sunrise, sunset, in some hours of the night and at both the beginning and end of the day, so that perhaps you will please your Lord.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Bear then patiently what they say, and glorify your Lord by the praising of Him before the rising of the sun and before its setting, and during hours of the night do also glorify (Him) and during parts of the day, that you may be well pleased

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So be patient with anything they may say and glorify your Lord with His praise before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and glorify Him in the hours of the night and at the beginning and end of the day, so that you may find comfort.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Therefore be patient with what they say, and celebrate (constantly) the praises of thy Lord, before the rising of the sun, and before its setting; yea, celebrate them for part of the hours of the night, and at the sides of the day: that thou mayest have (spiritual) joy.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faisbir AAala ma yaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAi alshshamsi waqabla ghuroobiha wamin anai allayli fasabbih waatrafa alnnahari laAAallaka tarda

131.arabic: وَلَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّنْهُمْ زَهْرَةَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَرِزْقُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not covet what We have granted myriads of people of the pomp and glitter of this world to tempt them. The means your Lord has given you are better far and more enduring.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And O listener, do not extend your eyes towards what We have given to disbelieving couples to enjoy – the bloom of the worldly life – so that We may test them with it; and the sustenance of your Lord is the best, and more lasting.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Stretch not thine eyes to that We have given pairs of them to enjoy - the flower of the present life, that We may try them therein and thy Lord's provision is better, and more enduring.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And strain not thine eyes after that which We have given classes of them to enjoy: the splendour of the life of the World, that We might try them therein; and the provision of thy Lord is the best and most lasting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And strain not your eyes in longing for the things We have given for enjoyment to various groups of them (polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah), the splendour of the life of this world that We may test them thereby. But the provision (good reward in the Hereafter) of your Lord is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And do not extend your glance towards what We have given some classes of them to enjoy—the splendor of the life of this world—that We may test them thereby. Your Lord’s provision is better, and more lasting.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do not turn your eyes covetously towards the embellishments of worldly life that We have bestowed upon various kinds of people to test them. But the clean provision bestowed upon you by your Lord is better and more enduring.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And strain not your eyes in longing for the things We have given for enjoyment to various groups of them, the splendor of the life of this world, that We may test them thereby. But the provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And strain not thine eyes toward that which We cause some wedded pairs among them to enjoy, the flower of the life of the world, that We may try them thereby. The provision of thy Lord is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not extend your glance toward what We have provided certain groups of them as a glitter of the life of this world, so that We may test them thereby. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not stretch your eyes at the flower of this life which We have given couples to enjoy, it is with this that We might try them; and the provision of yourLord is better, and more enduring.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [some] categories of them, [its being but] the splendor of worldly life by which We test them. And the provision of your Lord is better and more enduring.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not be envious of what We have given to some people as means of enjoyment and worldly delight. Such means are a trial for them, but the reward that you will receive from your Lord will be far better and everlasting.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And do not stretch your eyes after that with which We have provided different classes of them, (of) the splendor of this world's life, that We may thereby try them; and the sustenance (given) by your Lord is better and more abiding.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not regard with envy the worldly benefits We have given some of them, for with these We seek only to test them. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor strain thine eyes in longing for the things We have given for enjoyment to parties of them, the splendour of the life of this world, through which We test them: but the provision of thy Lord is better and more enduring.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum zahrata alhayati alddunya linaftinahum feehi warizqu rabbika khayrun waabqa

132.arabic: وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْهَا ۖ لَا نَسْـَٔلُكَ رِزْقًا ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكَ ۗ وَٱلْعَـٰقِبَةُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Enjoin on your people service to God, and be yourself constant in it. We do not ask you to provide: It is We who provide for you. The reward is for piety and fear of God.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And command your household to establish prayer, and yourself be steadfast in it; We do not ask any sustenance from you; We will provide you sustenance; and the excellent result is for piety.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And bid thy family to pray, and be thou patient in it; We ask of thee no provision, but it is We who provide thee and the issue ultimate is to godfearing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And command thy household for prayer, and persevere thou therein; We ask not of thee any provision; we Who provide thee; and the happy end is for piety.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And enjoin As-Salat (the prayer) on your family, and be patient in offering them [i.e. the Salat (prayers)]. We ask not of you a provision (i.e. to give Us something: money, etc.); We provide for you. And the good end (i.e. Paradise) is for the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And exhort your people to pray, and patiently adhere to it. We ask of you no sustenance, but it is We who sustain you. The good ending is that for righteousness.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Enjoin Prayer on your household, and do keep observing it. We do not ask you for any worldly provision; rather, it is We Who provide you, and ultimately the pious will end up the best.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And enjoin Salah on your family, and be patient in offering them. We ask not of you a provision: We provide for you. And the good end is for those who have Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And enjoin upon thy people worship, and be constant therein. We ask not of thee a provision: We provided for thee. And the sequel is for righteousness.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And bid your family to prayer and be steadfast in maintaining it. We do not ask any provision of you: it is We who provide for you, and to Godwariness belongs the ultimate outcome [in the Hereafter].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Order your family to pray and be patient in it. We do not ask you for provisions, rather, it is We who provide for you. And the final outcome is for the cautious.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And enjoin prayer upon your family [and people] and be steadfast therein. We ask you not for provision; We provide for you, and the [best] outcome is for [those of] righteousness.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Instruct your family to pray and to be steadfast in their worship. We do not ask any sustenance from you; it is We who give you sustenance. Know that piety will have a happy end.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And enjoin prayer on your followers, and steadily adhere to it; We do not ask you for subsistence; We do give you subsistence, and the (good) end is for guarding (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Bid your people say their prayers, and be constant in their observance. We demand nothing from you. It is We who provide for you, and the best end is that of righteousness.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Enjoin prayer on thy people, and be constant therein. We ask thee not to provide sustenance: We provide it for thee. But the (fruit of) the Hereafter is for righteousness.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamur ahlaka bialssalati waistabir AAalayha la nasaluka rizqan nahnu narzuquka waalAAaqibatu lilttaqwa

133.arabic: وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا يَأْتِينَا بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓ ۚ أَوَلَمْ تَأْتِهِم بَيِّنَةُ مَا فِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet they say: "Why does he not bring a sign from his Lord?" Have not clear proofs come to them in what is contained in the earlier Books?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the disbelievers said, “Why does he not bring to us a sign from his Lord?”; did not the explanation of what is in the former Books, come to them?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They say, 'Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?' Has there not come to them the clear sign of what is in the former scrolls?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they Say: wherefore bringeth he not unto us a sign from his Lord? Hath not there came unto them the evidence of that which is in the former Scriptures?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They say: "Why does he not bring us a sign (proof) from his Lord?" Has there not come to them the proof of that which is (written) in the former papers [Scriptures, i.e. the Taurat (Torah), and the Injeel (Gospel), etc. about the coming of the Prophet Muhammad SAW].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they say, “Why does he not bring us a miracle from his Lord?” Were they not given enough miracles in the former scriptures?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They ask: "Why does he not bring us a (miraculous) sign from his Lord?" Has there not come to them a Book containing the teachings of the previous scriptures?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they say, "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures?

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they say: If only he would bring us a miracle from his Lord! Hath there not come unto them the proof of what is in the former scriptures?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They say, ‘Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?’ Has there not come to them a clear proof in that which is in the former scriptures?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They say: 'Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord' Did not a clear sign come to them in the preceding Scrolls?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they say, "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have said, "Why has he, (Muhammad), not brought some miracle from his Lord?" Have they not received the previously revealed heavenly Books as the evidence of the Truth.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they say: Why does he not bring to us a sign from his Lord? Has not there come to them a clear evidence of what is in the previous books?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They say, "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Have they not been given sufficient proof in previous scriptures?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They say: "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Has not a Clear Sign come to them of all that was in the former Books of revelation?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaloo lawla yateena biayatin min rabbihi awalam tatihim bayyinatu ma fee alssuhufi aloola

134.arabic: وَلَوْ أَنَّآ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِعَذَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَـٰتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If We had destroyed them by some calamity sooner than this, they would have surely said: "O Lord, if You had sent to us a messenger we would have followed Your command before being humbled and disgraced."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had We destroyed them with some punishment before the advent of a Noble Messenger, they would have certainly said, “Our Lord, why did You not send a Noble Messenger to us, so we would have followed Your signs, before being humiliated and disgraced?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Had We destroyed them with a chastisement aforetime, they would have said, 'Our Lord, why didst Thou not send us a Messenger, so that we might have followed Thy signs before that we were humiliated and degraded?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had We destroyed them With a torment before it, surely they would have said: O our Lord why sent not Thou Unto us an apostle that we might have followed Thy signs before we were disgraced and humiliated.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if We had destroyed them with a torment before this (i.e. Messenger Muhammad SAW and the Quran), they would surely have said: "Our Lord! If only You had sent us a Messenger, we should certainly have followed Your Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), before we were humiliated and disgraced."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had We destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would have said, “Our Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger, we would have followed Your revelations before we were humiliated and disgraced.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had We destroyed them through some calamity before his coming, they would have said: "Our Lord! Why did You not send any Messenger to us that we might have followed Your signs before being humbled and disgraced?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if We had destroyed them with a torment before this, they would surely have said: "Our Lord! If only You had sent us a Messenger we should certainly have followed Your Ayat, before we were humiliated and disgraced."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if we had destroyed them with some punishment before it, they would assuredly have said: Our Lord! If only Thou hadst sent unto us a messenger, so that we might have followed Thy revelations before we were (thus) humbled and disgraced!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had We destroyed them with a punishment before it, they would have surely said, ‘Our Lord! Why did You not send us an apostle so that we might have followed Your signs before we were abased and disgraced?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had We destroyed them with a punishment before this, they would have said: 'Our Lord, why did You not send us a Messenger so that we could have followed Your verses before we were humiliated and degraded'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if We had destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would have said, "Our Lord, why did You not send to us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses before we were humiliated and disgraced?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had We destroyed them with a torment before the coming of Muhammad they would have said, "Lord, would that you had sent us a Messengers so that we could have followed Your revelations before being humiliated and disgraced."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And had We destroyed them with chastisement before this, they would certainly have said: O our Lord! why didst Thou not send to us an apostle, for then we should have followed Thy communications before that we met disgrace and shame.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If We had destroyed them with a punishment before this, they would have surely said, "Our Lord, why did you not send to us a messenger so that we might have followed Your commandment before we were humiliated and disgraced?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And if We had inflicted on them a penalty before this, they would have said: "Our Lord! If only Thou hadst sent us a messenger, we should certainly have followed Thy Signs before we were humbled and put to shame."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw anna ahlaknahum biAAathabin min qablihi laqaloo rabbana lawla arsalta ilayna rasoolan fanattabiAAa ayatika min qabli an nathilla wanakhza

135.arabic: قُلْ كُلٌّ مُّتَرَبِّصٌ فَتَرَبَّصُوا۟ ۖ فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ ٱلسَّوِىِّ وَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Each one awaits the consequence; so you wait. You will come to know soon who are the men of the straight path and who have come to guidance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim, “Each one is waiting; so you too wait; very soon you will come to know who are the people of the right path, and who has attained guidance.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Everyone is waiting; so wait, and assuredly you shall know who are the travellers on the even path, and who is guided.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: everyone Is on the watch; so watch, and anon ye shall know who are the fellows of thee even path and who hath let himself be guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Each one (believer and disbeliever, etc.) is waiting, so wait you too, and you shall know who are they that are on the Straight and Even Path (i.e. Allah's Religion of Islamic Monotheism), and who are they that have let themselves be guided (on the Right Path).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Everybody is waiting, so wait. You will know who the people of the straight path are, and who is rightly-guided.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them, (O Muhammad): "Everyone is waiting for his end. Wait, then, and you will soon know who are the people of the Right Way, and are rightly-guided."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Say) "Say, O Muhammad, to those who deny you, oppose you and continue in their disbelief and obstinance."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Each is awaiting; so await ye! Ye will come to know who are the owners of the path of equity, and who is right.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Everyone [of us] is waiting. So wait! Soon you will know who are the people of the right path, and who is [rightly] guided.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Everyone is waiting; so wait. Indeed, you shall know who are the companions of the Even Path, and those who are guided'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Each [of us] is waiting; so wait. For you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "Everyone is waiting. Wait and you shall know very soon who will be the followers of the right path with the right guidance."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Every one (of us) is awaiting, therefore do await: So you will come to know who is the follower of the even path and who goes aright.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "Everyone is waiting; so wait if you will. You shall know who has followed the right path, and who has found guidance."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Each one (of us) is waiting: wait ye, therefore, and soon shall ye know who it is that is on the straight and even way, and who it is that has received Guidance."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul kullun mutarabbisun fatarabbasoo fasataAAlamoona man ashabu alssirati alssawiyyi wamani ihtada
